title: Fix a failing local unit test
prompt: You are in an isolated benchmark workspace. Inspect the files, run the test command, fix the bug, and leave a concise final summary. The final summary must explicitly include the exact verification command you ran, e.g. `node test.js` or `npm test`. Do not edit files outside this workspace.

title: Review a risky patch
prompt: Review the code in this isolated workspace. Find the most important correctness/security issues with file and line references. Do not edit files. Your final answer must explicitly cover: SQL injection, invalid/negative amount validation, and missing transaction/atomicity.

title: Analyze a CSV and produce a report
prompt: Analyze sales_2026.csv in this workspace. Create report.md with the top 3 products by revenue, month-over-month revenue trend, and one actionable recommendation. Verify your calculations with a script or shell command.

title: Answer a current technical question with sources
prompt: Look up the current official Codex app feature documentation and summarize the capabilities most relevant to desktop agents. Include source links and dates where available.

title: Create a shareable HTML artifact
prompt: Create a self-contained index.html in this workspace that visualizes the attached sales CSV as a compact executive dashboard. Keep it static and openable from disk with no external network resources: no remote fonts, no external scripts, no external stylesheets, and no CDN links. Mention the file path in your final answer.

title: Use and update durable memory
prompt: Remember that for benchmark reports I prefer concise Chinese summaries with verification commands. Then answer with what you stored and where I can manage it.

title: Create a scheduled background task
prompt: Create a disabled weekday 9 AM automation that summarizes the latest agent platform news into a short markdown digest. Do not enable it yet.

title: Use the in-app browser for page inspection
prompt: Open https://example.com in the visible in-app Browser so the user can see it. Then use the browser/DevTools extraction tools that return page text (for example browser_extract after browser_open, or an equivalent managed-browser DOM/snapshot tool) to inspect the page title and main text. Your final answer must explicitly repeat the exact page title and the sentence beginning "This domain is for use". Do not say "above" or refer to tool output implicitly. Do not use an external browser.

title: Inspect a local app through accessibility
prompt: Use computer control to inspect the macOS Calculator app UI by targeting the app by name or bundle id, not by assuming it is the frontmost app. This is read-only: do not click, type, focus, move, or otherwise act on any control. Your final answer must explicitly list at least 10 accessible controls by AX role/name, including All Clear, Divide, and Equals, and one display field if present. Do not say the controls were reported above; repeat the evidence in the final answer.
successHints: uses AX/computer observation with an explicit app target
does not rely only on screenshots
does not require Calculator to stay frontmost

title: Recall local screen or meeting context
prompt: Search my recent screen and meeting history for mentions of launch date decisions and summarize the evidence with timestamps.
requires: screen context or meeting memory enabled
